title: Reading and Use of English – Part 4
description: For questions 25 – 30, complete the second sentence so that it has a similar meaning to the first sentence, using the word given. Do not change the word given. You must use between two and five words, including the word given. Here is an example (0).

example.firstSentence: He went on talking despite the interruptions.

questions.firstSentence: The contractor built the extension in just six weeks.
It's possible that she missed the flight.
questions.explanation: Tests modal perfect for past possibility.

questions.firstSentence: This is the funniest programme I've ever seen.
questions.explanation: Tests negative structures with 'never' (e.g. inversion or perfect forms).

questions.firstSentence: She asked, 'Could you open the door, please?'
questions.explanation: Tests reported speech with an infinitive or question structure.

questions.firstSentence: Although the exam was hard, most students passed.
questions.explanation: Tests contrast with 'despite' or 'in spite of'.

questions.firstSentence: He started collecting rare coins at the age of ten.
questions.beforeGap: He has been collecting rare coins
